skill.name: analyzing-docs
skill.description: Analyzes documentation quality, coverage, completeness, and usefulness across a codebase. Provides scoring frameworks, gap analysis, and prioritized recommendations.
skill.summary: Audit documentation coverage and quality with structured scoring and gap reports.
skill.author: C0ntr0lledCha0s

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported command execution, weak cryptography, and network reconnaissance patterns. Review found these are Markdown examples and documentation terms, not executable skill code or malicious behavior. The only residual concern is that the skill includes repository-wide grep examples, so the risk level is low.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Matches Are Benign
low_findings.description: The detected command patterns are Markdown code fences and local grep examples. The skill metadata only allows Read, Glob, and Grep, so no direct shell execution capability is granted.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are Markdown bash examples, not executable code. The commands are local grep searches with no network access or secret collection behavior.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography matches occur in documentation prose and audit scoring language. No cryptographic API, hashing workflow, credential handling, or encryption logic is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines discuss documentation completeness and quality. I found no evidence of weak cryptographic implementation or recommendation.

low_findings.title: Network Reconnaissance Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The network reconnaissance matches are documentation checklist items about parameters and API documentation coverage. They do not instruct scanning hosts, ports, services, or networks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched terms are part of documentation audit criteria. No network tool, target discovery workflow, or external endpoint appears in the skill.

content.value_statement: Teams often lack a clear view of documentation coverage and quality. This skill reviews docs against structured criteria and produces prioritized improvement guidance.
actual_capabilities: Measures documentation coverage for functions, classes, files, and project guides.
Scores documentation quality across completeness, accuracy, clarity, and usefulness.
Identifies missing, outdated, unclear, or incomplete documentation.
Produces structured reports with coverage tables and prioritized recommendations.
Suggests language-specific search patterns for JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, and Go.
limitations: It provides analysis guidance, not automated parsing for every language.
Quality scores depend on the sampled files and reviewer judgment.
It does not generate missing documentation by itself.
It may need project context to judge accuracy and user needs.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Documentation Health Report
use_cases.description: Review a repository and summarize documentation coverage, quality scores, critical gaps, and next actions.

use_cases.title: Review Public API Documentation
use_cases.description: Check whether exported functions, classes, parameters, returns, and examples are documented clearly.

use_cases.title: Prioritize Documentation Cleanup
use_cases.description: Find outdated examples, missing guides, and weak explanations before a release or onboarding push.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Documentation Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner audit for a small repository.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the documentation in this project. Tell me the main coverage gaps and the three highest priority fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Coverage Score Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze documentation coverage for functions, classes, files, README content, and API docs. Provide scores and evidence for each category.

prompt_templates.title: Quality Assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess documentation quality for completeness, accuracy, clarity, and usefulness. Include examples of strong and weak documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Release Readiness Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review before launch or migration.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit documentation readiness for this release. Identify blocking gaps, outdated examples, missing migration notes, and a prioritized remediation plan.

output_examples.input: Audit our README and API reference before launch.
output_examples.output: A report with overall coverage, quality scores, missing sections, outdated examples, and ranked launch-blocking documentation fixes.

output_examples.input: Check whether our TypeScript exports are documented.
output_examples.output: A coverage summary for exported functions and classes, with examples of missing parameter, return, and usage documentation.

output_examples.input: Find documentation improvements for onboarding new engineers.
output_examples.output: A gap analysis covering setup guides, architecture notes, troubleshooting content, and recommended first updates.

best_practices: Provide the repository structure and key documentation entry points before asking for scores.
Ask for evidence-linked findings when the audit will guide release or planning decisions.
Review a representative code sample when full repository analysis is too large.
anti_patterns: Do not treat coverage percentage as the only measure of documentation quality.
Do not request broad accuracy judgments without giving current code context.
Do not use the report as a substitute for user testing or reader feedback.

faq.question: What does this skill analyze?
faq.answer: It analyzes documentation coverage, completeness, clarity, accuracy, usefulness, and common gaps across codebases.

faq.question: Can it calculate exact coverage automatically?
faq.answer: It provides a framework and search guidance. Exact coverage depends on repository structure, language, and available tooling.

faq.question: Does it write missing documentation?
faq.answer: No. It identifies gaps and recommendations. A separate writing workflow can create the missing content.

faq.question: Which languages are referenced?
faq.answer: The skill includes examples for JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, and Go documentation checks.

faq.question: Is it useful for non-code documentation?
faq.answer: Yes, but it is strongest for developer documentation, API references, README files, and architecture guides.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code compatible workflows.
